title: Time-capsule recording — Jan 2 2020 'recording with my life'
html:
12:52 monologue parked in Dad's truck at the Albertsons / Miller Paint lot on New Year's morning 2020 — Yahir, age 16, calls it "a time capsule of sorts." Drifts across friends-and-girls regret, the coming senior year, college doubt, John Mauldin economics, dad and mom tension, a recurring deja vu paradox he's been chewing on at the clubhouse west, and the simulation question. One of the densest single nodes in the grants pass teen era corpus.

TL;DR

  • New Year's 2020 self-address from a parked truck — Yahir at 16 talking to "the boys" about looking out for each other, lamenting the Alicia/Kai situation isn't tripping him out, and noting he's never had a girlfriend / first kiss while still being "confident, nice with it."
  • Reads john mauldin economics and senses a 2020 downturn coming ("big shit might happen this year") — same broker reading list from the March 2019 broker conversation. Filed under financial freedom / scenario thinking.
  • Relitigates the college decision in real time: "why waste time, waste of money — what are you learning?" then reverses to "going to college might be the move — meet people, change the world." This is Yahir's own college admissions ambivalence on tape, six months before osu commitment.
  • Family tension named obliquely — "we'll see what's to come with your mom… one of those under-the-carpet situations… is our life slipping away from us?" — earliest first-person mention of the marriage-strain thread that runs through alcoholism in the family and Dad leaves to Mazatlan.
  • Names the deja vu paradox / simulation thinking he's been doing — "if this is a simulation y'all did a really goddamn good job" — and concludes "fulfillment is people for me," which is the seed of frequency as belonging / leader as network not iq.

Key quote

"Shit's coming bro, and you fucking said this before. Shit is fucking coming. It's fucking scary. Before you know it, bro — big grown-ass dude fucking next year next fucking year, bro."

Why it matters in the graph

  • This is teen Yahir installing his own memento mori decision filter before the COVID lockdown / dad-leaves arc kicks in two months later. He's already running the "is life slipping away?" check on himself.
  • Spans almost every concept-cluster in the vault in one recording: family, mortality, college, simulation, finance, friendship, romance — a single high-density node worth treating as a synthesis input.
  • The line "I was envisioning being a chill fucking [hybrid] — not like Elon Musk / Steve Jobs / Jeff Bezos hype note" directly contradicts the founder archetype energy of his later notes — useful tension to name.

Open questions

  • Did the predicted 2020 economic event actually pattern-match COVID for Yahir in retrospect? Worth pairing with covid pandemic era.
  • The Alicia/Kai/Leisha thread surfaces here, in 2020 01 23 casey, and in 2020 03 14 voice memo 1927 — candidate for a teen-romance synthesis page.
